career.title: AI Automation Engineer -- n8n + Claude Code
career.skills: n8n, Claude API, Claude Code, OpenAI, Google Gemini
career.match: This is literally your stack. n8n workflows + Claude Code + API integrations. Full-time (30+ hrs/week), contract-to-hire. Your DuberyMNL pipeline is the portfolio proof.

career.title: AI Automation Integrator -- Real Estate Ops
career.match: Real estate agent-to-coordinator automation. Your TDCX lead qualification + n8n skills fit perfectly. They specifically list Claude and n8n as tools.

career.title: AI Automation Specialist (Claude Code + n8n)
career.match: Starts with paid consultation, then ongoing build. Low risk entry. Your skill set matches exactly -- this is what you do every session.

career.title: Claude Bot Setup and Training
career.skills: Claude API, bot configuration, training docs
career.match: Set up a Claude bot and train the client. You could do this in a weekend based on your Claude Code expertise.

ai_news.title: Claude Computer Use + Dispatch shipped
ai_news.summary: Anthropic's biggest week: Claude can now control your Mac -- open apps, browse the web, fill spreadsheets. Dispatch lets you assign tasks from your phone and Claude runs them on your desktop while you're away. macOS only, Pro/Max subscribers.
ai_news.insight: You already run Claude Code via VSCode tunnel. Dispatch could complement that -- assign tasks from phone during commute, come back to finished work. Computer Use is macOS only for now but shows where agentic is heading.

ai_news.title: Claude Code Worktrees: ship 3 features at once
ai_news.summary: New worktree feature lets you run multiple Claude Code agents on separate git branches simultaneously. Each works in isolation, no merge conflicts until you're ready.
ai_news.insight: You're already using parallel agents for DuberyMNL (prompt writing, image gen). Worktrees would let you run dashboard + DuberyMNL + portfolio agents all at once without stepping on each other.

ai_news.title: MAI-Image-2: Microsoft's #3 AI image gen
ai_news.summary: Microsoft released MAI-Image-2, now ranked #3 on Arena leaderboard. Key wins: accurate text in images (no more gibberish), enhanced photorealism, detail density. Available in MAI Playground, rolling out to Copilot + Bing.
ai_news.insight: Text-in-image is the holy grail for your ad pipeline. If MAI-Image-2 handles headline text better than NB2/kie.ai, it could solve the branding consistency issue. Worth testing for DuberyMNL creatives.

ai_news.title: OpenAI offering 17.5% guaranteed return to investors
ai_news.summary: OpenAI is offering investors guaranteed 17.5% returns to raise capital for enterprise AI scaling. Aggressive move to lock in funding and partners.
ai_news.insight: Shows how much money is flowing into AI infrastructure. The companies building on top of these models (like you) are the ones who benefit from the spending war.

local_news.title: Water interruptions in Pasig and Taguig
local_news.summary: Taguig (Bagumbayan) had water cuts March 24-25, 10pm-4am. Pasig (Bagong Ilog) scheduled March 26-27, 10pm-4am. Pinagbuhatan also affected March 26-27.
local_news.action: Stock up water before your shift tonight in case Pasig gets hit. Check Maynilad advisories.

local_news.title: Summer officially started March 23
local_news.summary: PAGASA declared the warm/dry season. Expect fair weather with isolated thunderstorms. Temperatures will gradually increase through April.
local_news.action: Good news for the Daet trip (Mar 28 - Apr 2). Pack sunscreen and hydration for Baby Jah. Afternoons will be hot.

local_news.title: Fuel/commodity trucks exempt from number coding
local_news.summary: MMC exempted fuel delivery trucks and basic commodity transport from truck ban and number coding. Aimed at stabilizing supply during fuel crisis.
local_news.action: Should help prevent fuel station shortages. Fill up early in the week anyway -- Tuesday is hike day.

local_news.title: Ride-hailing drivers 2-day strike
local_news.summary: Thousands of Grab/InDrive drivers pledged a 2-day booking boycott starting March 23 over fare cuts and fuel costs.
local_news.action: If Arabelle uses Grab, expect longer wait times and surge pricing this week. Mototaxi or own vehicle is safer bet.

youtube_highlights.title: Claude Code MASSIVE Update! OS, Computer Use, /Schedule & More
youtube_highlights.summary: Full walkthrough of all new Claude Code features: Computer Use, Dispatch, Cowork, /schedule command. Best overview of the week's updates.

youtube_highlights.title: Top 5 AI News March 24th
youtube_highlights.summary: Quick 2-min roundup: OpenAI investor deals, Claude Computer Use, ChatGPT library feature, AI math breakthroughs, AI-assisted physics papers.

youtube_highlights.title: Microsoft's MAI-Image-2: #3 AI Image Generator
youtube_highlights.summary: Deep dive on MAI-Image-2 capabilities: photorealism, text rendering, detail density. Comparison with top models. Free in MAI Playground.

hardware.title: Raspberry Pi AI HAT+ 2 (Hailo-10H)
hardware.summary: Released Jan 2026. 40 TOPS inference (INT4), 8GB dedicated LPDDR4X memory. Pairs with Pi 5 for serious edge AI -- generative tasks + vision + sensor input without cloud dependency.
hardware.insight: At ~P5,000-7,000 for the full setup (Pi 5 + HAT+ 2), this is your entry point to local inference. Could run a baby monitor AI or local smart home agent.

hardware.title: 7 tiny AI models that run on Pi
hardware.summary: Meta Llama 3.1 8B, Qwen3-8B, GLM-4-9B can run on Pi 5 at 15+ tokens/sec with quantization. No GPU needed. Practical for on-device chatbots, document processing, home automation.
hardware.insight: Local LLM on a Pi means privacy-first AI for your family dashboard -- Baby Jah monitoring, receipt scanning, voice commands. All offline, no API costs.

hardware.title: Edge AI in 2026: bigger than you think
hardware.summary: On-device intelligence is growing fast across IoT, sensors, and personal devices. Companies shifting from cloud-first to edge-first for latency, privacy, and cost.
hardware.insight: Edge AI knowledge adds another dimension to your portfolio. 'I build cloud agents AND edge deployments' is a rare combo on Upwork.

life_hacks.title: Fuel crisis: fill up before Tuesday
life_hacks.body: Another P6.47/L gas hike and P11.88/L diesel hike expected this week. Premium gas already hit P100/L mid-March. Fill your scooter and the Vios before Tuesday when hikes take effect. For the Daet trip, budget P4,000-5,000 for fuel (round trip ~680km).

life_hacks.title: Toddler meal variety for Jah (21 months)
life_hacks.body: At 21 months, Jah can eat most family food cut into small pieces. Mix veggies into rice or pasta he already likes. Try kalabasa, carrots, broccoli. Tofu and eggs are cheap protein. 3 meals + 2 snacks per day. Don't replace meals with milk -- food first.

life_hacks.title: Chicken week: 40% cheaper than pork
life_hacks.body: Chicken at P180-220/kg vs pork liempo at P310-370/kg. Adobo, tinola, fried chicken all stretch the budget. DA is also eyeing P50/kg cap on imported rice -- watch for that.

life_hacks.title: Water interruption prep
life_hacks.body: Pasig water cuts scheduled Mar 26-27 (10pm-4am). Fill containers before your shift. If you're in Bagong Ilog or Pinagbuhatan area, stock extra for Baby Jah's bath and formula.

parenting.title: 21-month milestones: independence phase
parenting.summary: At 21 months, Jah is walking confidently, possibly running. He should be using a spoon on his own, saying 10-50 words, and starting to assert independence (saying 'no', tantrums). This is all normal development.
parenting.tip: Let Jah do things himself even if it's messy -- eating with a spoon, picking clothes, helping with small tasks. It builds confidence and motor skills.

parenting.title: Toddler meals: 3 meals + 2 snacks
parenting.summary: At 21 months, Jah should eat what the family eats (cut smaller). 3 meals + 2 snacks daily. Focus on calcium (milk, cheese), iron (meat, leafy greens), and fiber (fruits, veggies). 1 cup veggies, 1 cup fruit, 3 servings of rice/bread per day.
parenting.tip: Jah already eats lugaw, pasta, pork, bread. Try adding more veggies -- steamed kalabasa, carrots, broccoli mixed into his rice. Tofu and eggs are cheap protein wins.

parenting.title: Picky eating is normal at this age
parenting.summary: Toddlers at 21 months often refuse foods they used to love. It's a phase -- they're testing boundaries. Offer variety, don't force. It can take 10-15 exposures before a toddler accepts a new food.
parenting.tip: If Jah rejects something, try again in a few days prepared differently. Don't replace meals with milk -- offer food first, milk after. Keep portions small so it's not overwhelming.

fuel.trend: Massive escalation in March. Diesel up P24/L in one week (Mar 10), another P12/L hike announced Mar 23. Premium gas hit P100/L mid-March. Caused by Middle East conflict disrupting Strait of Hormuz oil supply.
fuel.tip: Fill up Monday before Tuesday hikes. For the Daet trip (Mar 28), fill the Vios tank completely before leaving Manila. Budget P4,000-5,000 for fuel round trip.

food.tip: DA eyeing P50/kg cap on imported rice. Chicken remains the budget protein winner at 40% less than pork. Inflation at 2.4% in February, driven by food prices. Stock up on rice and eggs before the Daet trip.

electricity.trend: Up P0.64/kWh from February. 200 kWh household pays ~P129 more. Your April bill (P5,503.15) is already pending. Summer demand will push rates higher in April-May.
electricity.tip: April Meralco bill is due Apr 3 -- P5,503.15 pending. Unplug idle chargers, minimize AC. Summer peak is coming. Fan + open windows at night saves P500-800/month vs AC.
